title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.title: Focaccia Genovese Recipe: Rosemary & Olive Oil Bread
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil. This easy recipe brings the taste of Italy to your kitchen. Perfect for baking enthusiasts!
longTailKeywords: how to make Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil
Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil
seo.h1: Authentic Focaccia Genovese with Rosemary & Olive Oil
h2Headings: Ingredients for Rosemary Olive Oil Focaccia
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil, golden brown and dimpled.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a slice of this rustic Focaccia Genovese, fragrant with rosemary and rich olive oil.

description: Savor the authentic taste of Liguria with this Focaccia Genovese, a perfectly chewy and airy bread infused with fragrant rosemary and rich olive oil. Its signature dimples hold pools of golden oil, creating an irresistible texture and flavor. This traditional Italian flatbread is ideal as an appetizer, a side dish, or simply enjoyed on its own.

ingredients.notes: 1/4 cup, plus more for pan and drizzling

instructions.title: Activate Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine warm water and yeast. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy. Add the fine sea salt and 2 tablespoons of olive oil, stirring to combine.

instructions.title: Mix and Knead Dough
instructions.text: Gradually add the flour to the wet ingredients, mixing until a shaggy dough forms. Transfer to a lightly floured surface and knead for about 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. It will be slightly sticky. Form it into a ball.

instructions.title: First Proof
instructions.text: Lightly oil a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning to coat. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Prepare for Baking Pan
instructions.text: Drizzle a generous amount of olive oil (about 2-3 tablespoons) into a 9x13 inch baking pan. Transfer the risen dough to the pan. Gently stretch and press the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it too much.

instructions.title: Second Proof and Dimpling
instructions.text: Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap and let it rest for another 30 minutes. Preheat your oven to 425F (220C). After resting, use your fingertips to create deep dimples all over the surface of the focaccia. Drizzle generously with more olive oil, sprinkle with chopped rosemary and flaky sea salt.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown on top and cooked through. If it browns too quickly, you can cover it loosely with foil for the last few minutes.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the focaccia from the oven and carefully transfer it to a wire rack to cool slightly. Drizzle with a little more fresh extra virgin olive oil if desired before slicing and serving warm.

tips: For an extra authentic touch, use a good quality Ligurian extra virgin olive oil. Its fruity notes will greatly enhance the focaccia's flavor.
Do not over-knead the dough after the first rise; gentle stretching is key to maintaining its airy texture. The dimples are crucial for holding the olive oil and creating characteristic pockets.
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh and warm. If you have leftovers, store them at room temperature in an airtight container for up to two days, or freeze for longer storage.
article:
Imagine the aroma wafting from a warm Italian kitchen: the earthy scent of fresh rosemary, the rich fruitiness of extra virgin olive oil, and the unmistakable promise of freshly baked bread. This is the sensory prelude to one of Italy's most beloved and iconic breads – Focaccia Genovese. More than just a flatbread, it's a testament to the simplicity and profound flavors of Ligurian cuisine, a celebrated jewel in the crown of Italian baking. This particular rendition, infused generously with rosemary and olive oil, elevates the experience, transforming humble ingredients into a sublime culinary delight that’s both comforting and sophisticated.

## What is Focaccia Genovese?
Focaccia Genovese hails from Genoa, the capital of Italy's Liguria region, nestled along the picturesque Italian Riviera. It stands apart from other focaccia types with its distinct characteristics: a remarkably thin and crisp base, an incredibly soft, airy, and chewy interior, and that signature dimpled surface, generously glistening with olive oil and often sprinkled with coarse sea salt. Unlike its thicker, breadier cousins found elsewhere in Italy, the Genovese style is celebrated for its delicate balance of texture and its intense olive oil flavor. Historically, it was often enjoyed for breakfast, dipped in coffee or milk, but today it’s a versatile staple – perfect as a snack, an appetizer, or an accompaniment to almost any meal. The essence of Focaccia Genovese lies in its few, high-quality ingredients, allowing each one to shine through, particularly the golden liquid gold that is extra virgin olive oil.

## Ingredients for Rosemary Olive Oil Focaccia
Crafting the perfect Rosemary Olive Oil Focaccia requires attention to ingredient quality. Here’s what you’ll need:

*   **Strong Bread Flour:** About 500g. Bread flour has a higher protein content, which is crucial for developing the gluten structure needed for focaccia’s characteristic chewiness. All-purpose flour can work, but the texture might be slightly less robust.
*   **Lukewarm Water:** About 350-400ml. The exact amount can vary based on your flour. It should be warm enough (around 105-115°F or 40-46°C) to activate the yeast without killing it.
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** 7g (one standard packet). This will give your focaccia its beautiful rise and airy crumb.
*   **Granulated Sugar:** 1 teaspoon. A tiny bit of sugar helps "feed" the yeast, encouraging it to activate quickly.
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** 10g (about 1.5 teaspoons) for the dough. Salt is essential for flavor and also plays a role in controlling yeast activity and gluten development.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** At least 100ml, plus more for the pan, brushing, and finishing. This is non-negotiable for Focaccia Genovese. Use a good quality, fruity olive oil – it’s a primary flavor component.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** 2-3 sprigs, needles finely chopped. Fresh is paramount here; dried rosemary simply won't impart the same vibrant, aromatic essence.
*   **Coarse Sea Salt:** For sprinkling on top before baking. This provides a delightful textural crunch and bursts of savory flavor.

## Step-by-Step Focaccia Genovese Instructions
Making this exquisite bread is a rewarding experience. Follow these steps for a perfect Rosemary Olive Oil Focaccia Genovese:

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the lukewarm water and sugar. Sprinkle the active dry yeast over the top. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until it becomes frothy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Pour in about 60ml of the extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. It will be very sticky – resist the urge to add more flour.
3.  **Knead (Lightly):** Transfer the dough to a lightly oiled surface. Knead gently for about 5-7 minutes. Because focaccia is a high-hydration dough, it won't be as smooth and elastic as regular bread dough, but it should become slightly less sticky and more cohesive. Alternatively, you can use a stand mixer with a dough hook.
4.  **First Rise:** Lightly oil a clean bowl. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap or a damp kitchen towel. Let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until it has doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare the Pan:** Generously coat a 9x13 inch (or similar size) baking pan with extra virgin olive oil. Don't be shy; this creates the crispy bottom crust.
6.  **Transfer and Stretch:** Gently deflate the risen dough and carefully transfer it to the oiled baking pan. Drizzle a little more olive oil over the top of the dough. Using your oiled fingertips, gently press and stretch the dough to fill the pan. If it springs back too much, let it rest for 10 minutes, then continue stretching.
7.  **Second Rise & Dimpling:** Once the dough roughly fills the pan, cover it again and let it rise for another 30-45 minutes. Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C) during the last 15 minutes of this rise. After the second rise, generously drizzle the dough with more extra virgin olive oil. With well-oiled fingers, press firmly all over the surface of the dough, creating deep dimples. Don't press all the way through.
8.  **Flavor and Bake:** Sprinkle the chopped fresh rosemary evenly over the dimpled dough, followed by the coarse sea salt. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the Focaccia Genovese is golden brown on top and deeply golden and crisp on the bottom.
9.  **Cool and Serve:** Once out of the oven, immediately drizzle with a final touch of high-quality extra virgin olive oil. Let it cool in the pan for a few minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool slightly further. Serve warm.

## Tips for Perfect Focaccia Genovese
Achieving that authentic Ligurian focaccia texture and flavor is all about technique and a few key considerations:

*   **Don't Skimp on Olive Oil:** This is perhaps the most crucial tip. Focaccia Genovese is drenched in olive oil – in the dough, in the pan, on top, and even a final drizzle after baking. It’s what gives it its characteristic rich flavor, tender crumb, and crispy crust. Use good quality extra virgin olive oil.
*   **High Hydration is Key:** Focaccia dough is meant to be very wet and sticky. Resist the urge to add more flour during mixing or kneading, as this will result in a dense, dry focaccia. Embrace the stickiness!
*   **Proper Proofing:** Allow ample time for both rises. The yeast needs time to work, creating those beautiful air pockets that make focaccia so light and airy. A warm, draft-free environment is ideal.
*   **Deep Dimples:** When dimpling, don't be afraid to press down firmly with oiled fingers. These indentations create pockets for the olive oil and salt to collect, resulting in bursts of flavor and a distinct texture.
*   **High Heat Baking:** Baking at a relatively high temperature ensures a quick rise in the oven (oven spring) and helps achieve that desirable crispy exterior while keeping the interior soft.
*   **Coarse Sea Salt:** The coarse salt on top is not just for flavor; it adds a delightful textural contrast and a pleasant crunch.

## Serving Suggestions for Genovese Focaccia
The beauty of Rosemary Olive Oil Focaccia Genovese lies in its versatility. It can be enjoyed in countless ways:

*   **As Is:** Simple and perfect, especially warm from the oven. It makes a fantastic breakfast, snack, or light lunch.
*   **With Dips:** Serve alongside a small bowl of your best extra virgin olive oil for dipping, perhaps with a splash of balsamic vinegar.
*   **Charcuterie & Cheese:** A wonderful addition to an antipasto platter, complementing cured meats and a variety of Italian cheeses.
*   **Soups & Salads:** Its airy texture makes it an excellent accompaniment to hearty soups or fresh, crisp salads.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice it horizontally and fill it with your favorite Italian deli meats, cheeses, roasted vegetables, or even a simple Caprese salad for an incredible sandwich.
*   **With Roasted Vegetables:** The savory notes of the focaccia pair beautifully with roasted peppers, zucchini, or eggplant.

## Variations & Substitutions for Focaccia
While the classic Rosemary Olive Oil Focaccia Genovese is sublime, there are many ways to adapt it to your taste:

*   **Toppings:** Get creative with other toppings! Thinly sliced red onions, cherry tomatoes (halved and pressed into the dough), olives (Kalamata or Castelvetrano), thinly sliced garlic, or a sprinkle of dried oregano can all be fantastic additions.
*   **Cheese:** A dusting of grated Parmesan or Pecorino Romano cheese can be added along with the coarse salt before baking for an extra layer of savory flavor.
*   **Herbs:** While rosemary is traditional, you could experiment with fresh thyme, oregano, or a mix of Italian herbs.
*   **Flour Blends:** For a slightly different texture and flavor, you could substitute a portion of the bread flour with semolina flour (for a coarser crumb) or whole wheat flour (for a nuttier, heartier bread).
*   **Infused Olive Oil:** For an even deeper flavor, use garlic-infused olive oil for your drizzles and pan coating.

Embracing the tradition of Italian baking, Focaccia Genovese is more than just a recipe; it's an experience. The process, from activating the yeast to pulling a golden-brown, aromatic slab from the oven, is a joy. The reward is a piece of culinary history, a taste of Liguria, and a truly satisfying bread that embodies the heart and soul of Italian cuisine. Whether you're a seasoned baker or new to the world of yeast, making this rosemary and olive oil infused Focaccia Genovese is a journey worth taking – your taste buds will thank you.
